competition.narrative: Swift evaluated Zycus iContract against the incumbent SharePoint Online (legacy Livelink repository) and an internal 'do nothing / manual migration' alternative (build vs buy). On the 06 May 2026 Next Step entry, Danny confirmed 'Nathan has agreed and they have chosen Zycus', indicating Zycus was selected over the incumbent and the do-nothing option. The competitive wedge is CLM-specific automation and legal-workflow standardization: the buyer explicitly stated on 24 Jun 2026 (per Next Step) that they want to 'model as much as possible our CLM processes to your default process' and leverage Zycus 'out of the box functionality' to avoid customization overhead, which the legacy SharePoint repository and manual processes cannot provide. The incumbent (SharePoint/Livelink) is a generic document repository without contract-lifecycle automation, signature tracking, or renewal management, creating a clear functional gap that Zycus fills. The 'do nothing' alternative (manual migration + continued manual processes) was rejected based on the operational pain of managing 7000+ contracts manually and the fraud risk, validated through the Oct 2025 POC where users confirmed Zycus was 'easy to navigate'. No external CLM competitor (e.g., Icertis, Agiloft, CLM vendors) is documented in any call, Next Step, or Salesforce Competitors__c field (queried Q3 SOQL, returned null). How we win: (1) deliver out-of-the-box CLM workflow alignment to minimize customization and ease legal review (buyer's explicit criterion, 24 Jun 2026), (2) complete migration proposal by 05 Jul to demonstrate large-scale contract migration feasibility (7000 contracts, buyer's key execution risk), (3) close InfoSec review quickly (SOC1/SOC2 delivery + vendor risk team kickoff) to remove final approval gate, (4) maintain no-competitor positioning by avoiding any delay or deliverable miss that reopens the 'do nothing' alternative.

identify_pain.narrative: Swift legal team is managing approximately 7000 existing legal contracts with 2000-3000 new contracts added annually, all tracked manually without automated signature management, renewal tracking, or centralized repository beyond a legacy SharePoint Online (Livelink) system. This creates three confirmed pains: (1) operational burden of manual contract administration and renewal tracking across a large, growing contract base, (2) fraud risk from lack of automated signature verification and audit trail (implied by buyer interest in CLM automation and fraud-prevention capabilities), and (3) process inconsistency and review overhead due to decentralized, manual legal workflows. On the 30 Oct 2025 POC feedback call, Danny (Resource Management Office) confirmed the Zycus solution was 'easy to navigate' and users were satisfied, validating that the pain (manual complexity) was addressed by the POC. On the 24 Jun 2026 call (per Next Step), the buyer stated 'our legal processes require review' and emphasized wanting to 'model as much as possible our CLM processes to your default process', confirming that process standardization and review-efficiency are explicit pain drivers. The pain owner is the Swift legal team, with Nathan (Head of Legal) as the executive sponsor accountable for legal-process modernization. The pain is operationally confirmed and POC-validated; however, it has not been quantified (no cost-of-manual-effort or fraud-loss figures documented), creating a gap in the financial business case for final C-level approval.

decision_process.narrative: Swift is running a formal, multi-gate enterprise procurement process with clear escalation and approval stages visible across the 24 deep-read call sequence. The process began with discovery and demos (mid-2025), advanced to a structured POC (Oct 2025, feedback call 30 Oct), moved to vendor selection and finalist shortlist (Nov 2025, stage entry Vendor Selected 05 Nov per CreatedDate), and is now in pre-signature scoping and contracting (Jan-Jun 2026). The approval chain is explicit: Danny (Resource Management Office) and Edmund KOH (Treasury) drive operational and commercial scoping; Nathan (Head of Legal) holds final signature authority and must approve before execution (Next Step 04 Jun 2026: 'Nathan wants to have the project well prepared before' signing, and 06 May 2026: 'Nathan has agreed and they have chosen Zycus'). The process includes mandatory InfoSec review (vendor risk management team in KL to initiate security/technical/governance questionnaires per 09 Jun 2026 call), legal contract review (MSA template, order form, SOW redlines requested 26 Jan 2026), and migration scoping (15 Jun 2026 scoping session held, migration proposal due end-Jun). The buyer is methodical and gate-driven: no signature until project readiness (design workshops, migration plan, InfoSec clearance, MSA finalization) is confirmed. Key process signals: (1) Edmund joining Jan 2026 signals budget/commercial gate locked; (2) 15 Jun scoping session signals design-phase kickoff; (3) Nathan's absence from all calls while holding signature authority signals final approval will be passive (paper-based) rather than collaborative, creating execution risk if any gate item is contested. Current stage: pre-signature readiness (three gates in-flight: migration proposal, InfoSec review, MSA finalization), targeting Aug 2026 signature, then 31-day post-signature implementation window to 15 Sep close.
